stem: A climate monitoring study measures daily temperature readings for 15 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±0.5°C of the calibration standard. The sample standard deviation is 5.0 °C (sample variance = 25.02). A second measurement method on 10 samples gives a sample variance of 15.71 (SD = 3.96 °C). The two methods may have different measurement precision.  Estimate the true population standard deviation of daily temperature readings and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Method 1: s=5.0, 95% CI for σ = [3.66, 7.89]. Method 2: s=3.96, 95% CI for σ = [2.95, 5.61]. Combined interval: [2.95, 7.89]. Point estimate: 4.51 °C.

solution_steps: Combined interval accounting for method uncertainty: [2.95, 7.89].
common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (5.0) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
Averaging the two sample variances without considering they may represent different measurement precisions
